project.name: Django Pet Management System
project.description: A Django-based pet management platform with Vue.js frontends (user-facing SPA and admin panel), supporting pet sales, adoption, foster care, boarding, health tracking, forum, chat, and big data analytics via Hive/HDFS/Spark.

nodes.name: changeEvent

nodes.summary: 退款申请模块的后台表格配置，定义退款申请数据的表格列、搜索条件、操作按钮及时间表格展示。

nodes.name: table.js
nodes.summary: 系统管理员模块的后台表格配置，定义管理员数据表的元数据、列字段（用户名、密码、角色、头像）、搜索条件、操作按钮及时间维度表格展示。

nodes.name: table.js
nodes.summary: 普通用户模块的后台表格配置，定义用户数据表的元数据、列字段（账号、密码、姓名、性别、手机号、收货地址、积分、头像、邮箱、状态）、搜索条件及操作按钮。

nodes.languageNotes: 包含跨表按钮（crossTable_0），关联宠物订单表实现在线购买功能。

nodes.summary: 布局菜单按钮组件，提供侧边菜单折叠与展开的切换功能，通过 inject 注入来自父布局的折叠状态和事件。

nodes.name: Notice.vue
nodes.summary: 空的通知占位组件，仅包含空模板，为未来通知功能预留位置。

nodes.name: RoleMenu.vue
nodes.summary: 用户角色菜单下拉组件，显示用户头像和用户名，通过 inject 接收角色菜单列表并处理菜单事件。

nodes.name: Weather.vue
nodes.summary: 空的天气占位组件，仅包含空模板，为未来天气显示功能预留位置。

nodes.name: CommonInfo.vue
nodes.summary: 通用内容展示组件，支持 Markdown 渲染和图片列表两种展示模式，用于弹框中展示文本或图片数据。

nodes.name: List.vue
nodes.summary: 通用列表页面主组件，集成了搜索筛选、分页、多选、增删改查、跨表操作、审核、支付、图表统计、AI 数据分析、提醒预警和论坛评论等功能，是后端管理系统的核心页面。
nodes.languageNotes: Vue 3 `<script setup>` 语法，大量使用 async/await 异步操作和 Element Plus UI 组件库。

nodes.name: Login.vue
nodes.summary: 登录页面主组件，支持账号密码登录、邮箱/手机验证码登录、人脸登录等多种认证方式，集成表单验证和角色选择功能。

nodes.name: Register.vue
nodes.summary: 注册页面主组件，支持邮箱或手机号注册，包含表单验证、验证码输入和角色关联功能，注册成功后跳转登录。

nodes.name: getShow_tableButtons
nodes.summary: 根据当前表格名和按钮名称判断该按钮是否对当前行显示，用于特殊表的条件性按钮展示。

nodes.name: actionEvent
nodes.summary: 表格行操作按钮的统一事件处理器，根据按钮类型分发到查看、编辑、删除、评论等功能。

nodes.name: discussReply
nodes.summary: 回复评论功能，弹出输入框引导用户输入回复内容后提交。

nodes.name: replyEvent
nodes.summary: 处理回复提交逻辑，构建回复数据对象并将其追加到原评论的 reply JSON 数组中，再调用更新接口保存到后端。

nodes.name: headerEvent
nodes.summary: 表头操作按钮的统一事件处理器，根据按钮类型分发到新增、批量删除、审核、支付、图表统计和 AI 分析等功能。

nodes.name: remove
nodes.summary: 单行删除功能，弹出确认对话框后调用删除 API 并刷新数据。

nodes.name: removes
nodes.summary: 批量删除功能，验证已选中数据后弹出确认对话框，调用批量删除 API 并刷新列表。

nodes.name: add
nodes.summary: 新增功能，打开编辑弹框并初始化为新增模式，传入默认数据、表名和路由参数。

nodes.name: edit
nodes.summary: 编辑功能，打开编辑弹框并初始化为更新模式，传入当前行数据供修改。

nodes.name: crossTableHander
nodes.summary: 跨表操作处理器，支持审核校验、支付校验、倒计时校验、次数限制和状态限制等多种前置条件判断，然后弹出对应的编辑或预约弹框。

nodes.name: showChart
nodes.summary: 图表展示功能，根据表名和标题查找匹配的图表配置数据并打开图表弹框。

nodes.name: sfsh
nodes.summary: 审核功能，验证选中数据的可审核状态后打开审核弹框，传入待审核数据和列配置。

nodes.name: pay
nodes.summary: 单行支付功能，验证审核状态后打开支付弹框。

nodes.name: pays
nodes.summary: 批量支付功能，验证选中数据的支付状态和审核状态后打开支付弹框。

nodes.name: remindEvent
nodes.summary: 数据提醒预警功能，遍历列配置中的提醒规则（数量/日期/文字），调用后端统计接口并在数据达到预警线时弹出通知。

nodes.name: tableCellClassName
nodes.summary: 表格行样式回调函数，为触发预警的数据行添加高亮样式类名。

nodes.name: forumreport
nodes.summary: 论坛举报处理功能，已处理的举报直接提示，否则打开编辑弹框进行状态更新。

nodes.name: aiAnalysis
nodes.summary: AI 数据分析功能，将选中或当前页数据拼接为提示词发送给 DeepSeek API 进行数据分析，结果以 Markdown 格式展示。

nodes.name: fetchData
nodes.summary: 数据拉取核心函数，根据当前分页参数、路由参数和搜索条件拼接请求参数，调用后端分页 API 获取列表数据。

nodes.name: handleLogin
nodes.summary: 表单登录处理函数，先执行表单校验，校验通过后调用登录 API，成功后执行登录后续事件。

nodes.name: loginSucessEvent
nodes.summary: 登录成功后的缓存与跳转处理，缓存 token、用户信息、头像、角色菜单等到 localStorage，然后跳转到首页。

nodes.name: registerEvent
nodes.summary: 注册表单提交处理函数，执行表单校验、密码一致性检查、邮箱验证码处理，调用注册 API 后跳转到登录页。

nodes.name: LoginButton.vue
nodes.summary: 登录页面的提交按钮组件，使用 Element Plus 的 el-button 渲染，触发表单 submit 事件进行登录。

nodes.name: LoginTitle.vue
nodes.summary: 登录页面的标题与 Logo 展示组件，通过 $projectImages.logo 显示 Logo 图片，展示项目名称。

nodes.name: Password.vue
nodes.summary: 密码输入框组件，通过 inject 从父组件获取 ruleForm，使用 Element Plus 密码输入框并支持密码显隐切换。

nodes.name: RegisterButtons.vue
nodes.summary: 注册入口按钮列表组件，通过 inject 获取注册选项列表和点击事件，遍历渲染多个注册角色入口。

nodes.name: Roles.vue
nodes.summary: 角色选择下拉框组件，通过 inject 获取角色列表和表单数据，当角色数大于 2 时显示角色选择器。

nodes.name: DetailPrice.vue
nodes.summary: 价格展示组件，以货币格式显示商品价格金额，支持 label 标签前缀。

nodes.name: DetailReversetime.vue
nodes.summary: 倒计时组件，基于 dayjs 和 Element Plus 的 el-countdown 实现截止时间倒计时，到期自动切换为已结束状态。
nodes.languageNotes: Vue <script setup> 组件，集成 dayjs 进行时间计算和 el-countdown 渲染倒计时。

nodes.name: DetailTag.vue
nodes.summary: 标签展示组件，根据 column.options 配置查找匹配项并用 el-tag 渲染，支持 type 样式区分。

nodes.name: DetailTitle.vue
nodes.summary: 标题展示组件，纯文本方式渲染传入的标题值。

nodes.name: DetailUserName.vue
nodes.summary: 用户名展示组件，支持匿名显示模式：当 data.isanon 为 1 时显示「匿名」而非真实用户名。

nodes.name: index.js
nodes.summary: DetailItem 组件 barrel 文件，使用 Vite 的 import.meta.glob 自动发现并全局注册 components 目录下的所有 Vue 组件。
nodes.languageNotes: 利用 Vite import.meta.glob 实现组件自动注册，避免手动逐个导入。

nodes.name: YyAIAnalyse.vue
nodes.summary: AI 情感分析组件，调用 DeepSeek API 对表单字段进行语义分析，将结果自动填充到文本域中。

nodes.name: YyAiEditor.vue
nodes.summary: AI 富文本编辑器组件，集成 AiEditor 库并配置 DeepSeek API，支持双向数据绑定和编辑状态控制。
nodes.languageNotes: 通过 CDN 加载的 AiEditor 库实现富文本编辑，需轮询检测全局 AiEditor 对象是否就绪。

nodes.name: YyFile.vue
nodes.summary: 通用文件上传组件，支持图片、文档、音频、视频等多种类型，集成图片识别（OCR/动植物/菜品等）和文件加密功能。

nodes.name: analyseEvent
nodes.summary: 异步分析事件，收集表单中所有 faceMatch 为 DS 的字段值拼接成提问文本，调用 DeepSeek API 获取分析结果并填充到目标字段。

nodes.name: initEditor
nodes.summary: 初始化 AiEditor 富文本编辑器，配置 DeepSeek API 密钥和模型，绑定 onChange 回调同步内容至表单数据。

nodes.name: handlePictureCardPreview
nodes.summary: 处理文件预览事件：图片全屏预览、音视频新窗口打开、文件触发下载。

nodes.name: handleSuccess
nodes.summary: 文件上传成功回调，更新本地文件列表和表单数据，若配置识图功能则触发图片识别。

nodes.name: encryptionEvent
nodes.summary: 文件加密事件，调用后端加密接口对已上传的文件进行加密处理，支持多类型加密格式。

nodes.name: recognizeEvent
nodes.summary: 图片识别事件入口，根据 faceMatch 类型（文字、动物、植物等）调用相应的图像识别 API 并将结果分发至对应的表单字段。

nodes.name: ocr
nodes.summary: OCR 识别结果赋值函数，将识别文本填入类型为「普」的表单字段。

nodes.name: common
nodes.summary: 通用识别结果赋值函数，根据字段类型（链接/描述/名称）将百科数据分发至对应表单字段。

nodes.name: car
nodes.summary: 汽车识别结果赋值函数，将车型、年份、百科链接等信息分发至对应的表单字段。

nodes.name: advancedGeneral
nodes.summary: 通用高级识别结果赋值函数，处理分类层级（一级/二级）、百科链接和描述信息的分发。

nodes.name: bodyNum

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: AGENT.md serves as the project's comprehensive developer guide, documenting the full architecture, directory structure, routing conventions, and coding standards across all layers. It explains how the Django backend serves both a user-facing Vue.js SPA and an admin panel SPA, each with its own Vite build pipeline and distinct UI. This document provides the essential mental model and vocabulary needed before exploring any code, making it the natural starting point for the tour.

tour.title: Configuration Foundation
tour.description: The Django project settings in dj2/settings.py define the database connection (MySQL with PyMySQL), installed middleware stack, CORS policies, session engine, and third-party integrations including email and Alipay keys. Alongside it, requirements.txt lists all Python dependencies -- Django 5.2, PyMySQL, OpenAI SDK, Baidu AI SDK, and Apache Hadoop components -- that establish the runtime environment every other layer depends on.

tour.title: Application Entry and Routing
tour.description: The manage.py script is the Django project's CLI entry point: it sets DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE, executes management commands, and starts the development server with automatic browser launch. The main/urls.py router then dynamically discovers every _v.py file in the main directory and registers its URL patterns automatically, while apps.py configures the application's display name. This convention-driven routing means adding a new API module requires creating only the _v.py file -- no manual route configuration needed.

tour.title: ORM Data Models
tour.description: The ORM data layer is the foundation of every business operation, with models.py defining 30+ Django model classes that map directly to MySQL database tables. The base model.py class provides generic CRUD operations (page, list, save, update, delete, statistics) inherited by all business models, while config_model.py stores system-level key-value configuration. These three files together have the highest fan-in in the entire codebase (34-37 imports each), confirming their role as the central dependency for all API views.
tour.languageLesson: Django ORM models define database table structure through Python class inheritance. Each model attribute maps to a database column, and the base model class (model.py) implements reusable query methods that every view calls, avoiding SQL repetition.

tour.title: Shared Utility Layer
tour.description: The utility layer provides shared infrastructure consumed by every API view: auth.py implements Base64 token-based authentication and session validation, codes.py defines standardized business status codes used in all API responses, common.py provides MD5 hashing, CustomJSONEncoder.py handles datetime and Decimal serialization for JSON responses, and baidubce_api.py wraps 15 Baidu Cloud AI services (face recognition, OCR, NLP, translation, image analysis). These files have fan-in scores of 32-36, making them the most depended-upon modules in the project -- every single API view imports them all.
tour.languageLesson: Python utility modules with high fan-in follow a stateless, function-oriented design pattern. Each module exports pure functions or constants without class overhead, making them import-safe from any context without initialization side effects.

tour.title: API View Pattern
tour.description: Each _v.py file in the main directory follows a consistent CRUD pattern: implementing default, page, list, save, add, update, delete, detail, info, thumbsup, and vote methods against its corresponding model. The Yonghu_v.py file demonstrates this pattern with 26 view functions handling user registration (with email verification), login, session management, and profile CRUD -- the most complete example in the codebase. Building on Steps 4 and 5, it imports models.py and all five utility modules, showing the complete dependency chain from HTTP request to database persistence.
tour.languageLesson: Django REST views in this project follow a function-based pattern rather than class-based views, with each endpoint mapped to a named function. The convention of appending '_v' to view module filenames enables automatic URL discovery by the router.

tour.title: General-Purpose API Gateway
tour.description: The schema_v.py file breaks the standard CRUD mold by providing nine general-purpose endpoints: WebSocket connections, file upload and download, AES/DES encryption and decryption, Baidu AI face recognition, AI chat, sentiment analysis, web crawling, and QR code generation. This file has the highest fan-out (9 imports) because it integrates more external capabilities than any other view. It demonstrates how the project extends beyond basic data CRUD to offer a full-service platform with AI, real-time communication, and security features.

tour.title: Request Middleware Pipeline
tour.description: The xmiddleware directory implements a two-stage request processing pipeline that intercepts every API call. The xparam middleware preprocesses incoming request parameters -- handling decoding, validation, and session ID extraction -- while xauth middleware validates the Base64 auth token for protected endpoints, returning standardized error codes from Step 5's codes.py when authentication fails. These middleware components sit between the URL router and view functions, enforcing security and data normalization consistently across all 34 API views.

tour.title: Database Schema
tour.description: The SQL schema file defines 33 database tables that physically store all application data: users and store staff, pets for sale and adoption, orders (pet, service, supplies, adoption, foster), service packages, health records, forum posts, chat messages, comments, favorites, and system configuration. Each table corresponds directly to a Django ORM model from Step 4 and an API handler from Step 6, forming a three-way traceability chain that makes the data flow easy to follow from HTTP request to disk.
tour.languageLesson: The SQL schema uses InnoDB engine with utf8mb4 encoding for full Unicode support. Foreign keys are handled at the application layer (Django ORM) rather than database-level constraints, a common pattern in Django projects that trades some referential integrity for deployment flexibility.

tour.title: Big Data Infrastructure
tour.description: The data infrastructure layer extends the application beyond MySQL into Hadoop-based analytics. The init.py CLI tool bootstraps databases from SQL schema files using Click commands, while initial_hive_database.py converts MySQL DDL into Hive-compatible DDL for running SQL-style analytics on Hadoop clusters. The hive_func.py module provides programmatic Hive query execution, supporting large-scale data processing pipelines that would be impractical in a standalone MySQL database.
tour.languageLesson: Hive DDL conversion requires mapping MySQL data types (VARCHAR, INT, DECIMAL, DATETIME) to Hive equivalents (STRING, INT, DOUBLE, TIMESTAMP). This project's converter also handles CREATE TABLE, INSERT, DROP statements using sqlparse, making it a bridge between operational (MySQL) and analytical (Hive) data stores.

tour.title: User-Facing Frontend SPA
tour.description: The user-facing single-page application is built with Vue 3, Vite, Element Plus, and Pinia. The main.js file bootstraps the app by creating and mounting the Vue instance with Pinia state management and Vue Router plugins. The router/index.js defines a Hash-mode router that dynamically loads page components based on user role permissions, while store/index.js manages login state, user profile, and menu caching in localStorage. The index.html loads Element Plus and ECharts via CDN for runtime code splitting, keeping the initial bundle small.
tour.languageLesson: Vue 3 single-file components with <script setup> provide a Composition API syntax that reduces boilerplate. The store uses Pinia's defineStore with the setup() style, enabling TypeScript-style state management without class decorators.

tour.title: Admin Frontend SPA
tour.description: The admin panel follows the same Vue 3 + Vite + Pinia architecture as the user frontend but targets administrative workflows. Its store/index.js manages admin user state and a menu version number that triggers UI refresh when role permissions change. The admin panel adds comprehensive CRUD table interfaces, ECharts dashboards, service chat components, and high-level business analytics views that the user-facing app omits. Both frontends share the same Django API backend (Steps 6-7) but present entirely different UIs optimized for their respective audiences.
